description: check JCL syntax before submitting to the mainframe
post a message to the team Slack channel
run a health check against the staging API endpoint
ask OpenAI to summarize the deployment log and email the summary
submit the job; if it passes, write the result to the database
validate the JCL locally, then submit via zosmf if it passes
run cargo build on the workspace and notify on failure
ask Gemini to draft a postmortem from the incident log
dsl:
flow "Draft Postmortem" v1.0.0

draft[cloud_ai: "Draft postmortem"] {
  provider: "gemini"
  modelId: "gemini-1.5-pro"
  prompt: "Draft a postmortem from the incident log"
  maxTokens: 1024
}

validate JCL; on pass, submit and then archive to s3; on fail, create a JIRA ticket
run a mock step for integration testing
use Claude to analyze the spool and create a JIRA issue with the findings

ssh to the production host and run the migration script

run the JCL validation model with low confidence thresholds

get job status via zosmf and write the result to the database

get the job status from zosmf and if it passes archive the output

use Claude to audit the change list and write the findings to the database
run an SSH command on the staging server and always clean up afterwards
validate JCL with the local model; always archive the validation result regardless of outcome
run a shell command and post the output to Slack on success only
ask OpenAI to translate the release notes to French
dsl:
flow "Translate Notes" v1.0.0

translate[cloud_ai: "Translate notes"] {
  provider: "openai"
  modelId: "gpt-4o"
  prompt: "Translate the release notes to French"
  maxTokens: 512
  temperature: 0.3
}

fetch with self-edge retry
dsl:
flow "retry-fetch" v1

fetch[action: "Fetch"] {
  adapter: "shell"
  actionId: "curl"
  args: "-fsSL https://example.com/a.tar.gz"
}

fetch.fail --> fetch
